resultRows.item: Mood & Brain DNA Report validated model
resultRows.geneticAnalysis: No validated package-specific mental-health, mood, cognitive, addiction, treatment-response, or calibrated PRS output is present in the local fixture.
resultRows.plainEnglishMeaning: This local run can document that model evidence is unavailable, but it cannot turn that absence into a diagnosis, current mental-state assessment, mental-health risk, cognitive result, treatment response, or health action.

resultRows.item: Symptoms, cognitive context, medication context, and professional review
resultRows.geneticAnalysis: Symptoms, current mental state, cognitive testing, neurologic exam, medications, pediatric context, school or work context, and professional interpretation are not supplied.
resultRows.plainEnglishMeaning: Genome-derived evidence alone is not enough to decide mood state, diagnosis, ADHD, dyslexia, addiction risk, cognitive status, therapy, medication, supplement, sleep intervention, school/work action, or all-clear questions.

resultRows.geneticAnalysis: The sample explains that reports analyze how DNA influences health, summarize results as a risk score or gauge, use percentile comparisons, and rank recommendations by impact and evidence. The introductory pages also state that reports do not diagnose health conditions and that genetics is only one part of health.
resultRows.plainEnglishMeaning: Observed public Mood & Brain sample-report structure only. Keep this separate from local genome evidence; it is not a personal mental-health, mood, addiction, eating-disorder, ADHD, dyslexia, cognitive, therapy, sleep, medication, supplement, school/work, treatment, or medical recommendation.

resultRows.geneticAnalysis: The sample summary says the report contains 23 genetic results and covers mental health, substance use and eating disorders, cognitive problems, and cognitive traits. The sample frames brain function as influenced by past experiences, current environment, diet, lifestyle, and DNA.
resultRows.plainEnglishMeaning: Observed public Mood & Brain sample-report structure only. Keep this separate from local genome evidence; it is not a personal mental-health, mood, addiction, eating-disorder, ADHD, dyslexia, cognitive, therapy, sleep, medication, supplement, school/work, treatment, or medical recommendation.

resultRows.geneticAnalysis: Visible mental-health cards include Seasonal Low Mood typical likelihood; Stress typical likelihood; Anxiety typical likelihood; Caffeine-Related Anxiety less likely; Low Mood more likely; Mood Swings less likely; Obsessive-Compulsive Tendencies typical likelihood; Teeth Grinding less likely; Psychological Trauma typical likelihood. The sample overview uses lower/typical/higher likelihood labels and plain-English subtitles for each card.
resultRows.plainEnglishMeaning: Observed public Mood & Brain sample-report structure only. Keep this separate from local genome evidence; it is not a personal mental-health, mood, addiction, eating-disorder, ADHD, dyslexia, cognitive, therapy, sleep, medication, supplement, school/work, treatment, or medical recommendation.

resultRows.groupTitle: Overview of results / Addictions And Eating Disorders
resultRows.item: Five addictions and eating-disorder result cards
resultRows.geneticAnalysis: Visible cards include Addictions typical likelihood; Alcohol Addiction less likely; Tobacco Addiction less likely; Cannabis Addiction less likely; Eating Disorders typical likelihood. The sample separates addiction/eating-disorder result cards from mental-health and cognitive cards.
resultRows.plainEnglishMeaning: Observed public Mood & Brain sample-report structure only. Keep this separate from local genome evidence; it is not a personal mental-health, mood, addiction, eating-disorder, ADHD, dyslexia, cognitive, therapy, sleep, medication, supplement, school/work, treatment, or medical recommendation.

resultRows.groupTitle: Overview of results / Cognitive Problems and Traits
resultRows.geneticAnalysis: Visible cognitive cards include Cognitive Decline typical likelihood; Brain Fog typical likelihood; Attention higher risk/more likely to have ADHD; Dyslexia more likely; Creativity higher; Memory Performance typical; Short-Term Memory typical; Processing Speed typical; Executive Function typical. The second overview page groups cognitive problems separately from cognitive traits.
genes: Cognitive problem and cognitive trait model cards
resultRows.plainEnglishMeaning: Observed public Mood & Brain sample-report structure only. Keep this separate from local genome evidence; it is not a personal mental-health, mood, addiction, eating-disorder, ADHD, dyslexia, cognitive, therapy, sleep, medication, supplement, school/work, treatment, or medical recommendation.

resultRows.geneticAnalysis: Typical likelihood of seasonal affective disorder; 43rd percentile; based on 90 genetic variants. The sample page says genetics may account for about 30% of differences in seasonality and lists family history, higher latitude, and low vitamin D as other risk factors.
resultRows.plainEnglishMeaning: Observed public Mood & Brain sample-report structure only. Keep this separate from local genome evidence; it is not a personal mental-health, mood, addiction, eating-disorder, ADHD, dyslexia, cognitive, therapy, sleep, medication, supplement, school/work, treatment, or medical recommendation.

resultRows.geneticAnalysis: Typical likelihood of feeling stressed; 38th percentile; based on 383,309 genetic variants. The sample page says up to 45% of differences in perceived stress may be attributed to genetics and explains HPA-axis stress-hormone context.
resultRows.plainEnglishMeaning: Observed public Mood & Brain sample-report structure only. Keep this separate from local genome evidence; it is not a personal mental-health, mood, addiction, eating-disorder, ADHD, dyslexia, cognitive, therapy, sleep, medication, supplement, school/work, treatment, or medical recommendation.

resultRows.geneticAnalysis: The sample says recommendations are prioritized by likely impact based on genetics plus the amount of scientific evidence supporting the recommendation. The recommendation pages show named recommendation blocks with condition-level impact and evidence ratings.
resultRows.plainEnglishMeaning: Observed public Mood & Brain sample-report structure only. Keep this separate from local genome evidence; it is not a personal mental-health, mood, addiction, eating-disorder, ADHD, dyslexia, cognitive, therapy, sleep, medication, supplement, school/work, treatment, or medical recommendation.

resultRows.geneticAnalysis: Visible rows include Attention impact 3/5 evidence 3/5; Low Mood impact 3/5 evidence 4/5; Psychological Trauma impact 3/5 evidence 3/5; Anxiety impact 4/5 evidence 4/5; Brain Fog impact 3/5 evidence 4/5; Addictions impact 3/5 evidence 3/5; Stress impact 4/5 evidence 4/5; Eating Disorders impact 3/5 evidence 3/5; Executive Function impact 3/5 evidence 3/5; Memory Performance impact 3/5 evidence 4/5; Short-Term Memory impact 3/5 evidence 3/5; Cognitive Decline impact 3/5 evidence 3/5; Teeth Grinding impact 4/5 evidence 4/5; Mood Swings impact 3/5 evidence 3/5; Tobacco Addiction impact 4/5 evidence 4/5; Alcohol Addiction impact 3/5 evidence 3/5; Creativity impact 3/5 evidence 3/5; Cannabis Addiction impact 3/5 evidence 3/5. The sample describes yoga, meditation, mindfulness, applied relaxation, massage, tai chi, biofeedback, and related practices across different rows.
resultRows.plainEnglishMeaning: Observed public Mood & Brain sample-report structure only. Keep this separate from local genome evidence; it is not a personal mental-health, mood, addiction, eating-disorder, ADHD, dyslexia, cognitive, therapy, sleep, medication, supplement, school/work, treatment, or medical recommendation.

resultRows.geneticAnalysis: The sample says mindfulness-based relaxation may improve attention more in people with your ANKK1 gene variant. The visible variant table shows ANKK1 rs1800497 GG with a two-dot evidence indicator.
resultRows.plainEnglishMeaning: Observed public Mood & Brain sample-report structure only. Keep this separate from local genome evidence; it is not a personal mental-health, mood, addiction, eating-disorder, ADHD, dyslexia, cognitive, therapy, sleep, medication, supplement, school/work, treatment, or medical recommendation.

resultRows.geneticAnalysis: The sample says people with your CELF4 gene variant may be more prone to stress and that relaxation techniques may help with PTSD by reducing stress. The visible variant table renders gene as '/', SNP rs11665070, genotype GA, with a two-dot evidence indicator.
resultRows.plainEnglishMeaning: Observed public Mood & Brain sample-report structure only. Keep this separate from local genome evidence; it is not a personal mental-health, mood, addiction, eating-disorder, ADHD, dyslexia, cognitive, therapy, sleep, medication, supplement, school/work, treatment, or medical recommendation.

resultRows.geneticAnalysis: The sample says your OXTR gene variant is associated with anxiety and issues with emotional attachment and that massage may help by boosting oxytocin. The visible variant table shows OXTR rs53576 AG with a two-dot evidence indicator.
resultRows.plainEnglishMeaning: Observed public Mood & Brain sample-report structure only. Keep this separate from local genome evidence; it is not a personal mental-health, mood, addiction, eating-disorder, ADHD, dyslexia, cognitive, therapy, sleep, medication, supplement, school/work, treatment, or medical recommendation.

resultRows.geneticAnalysis: The sample says stress may have a stronger impact on executive function in people with your COMT gene variant. The visible variant table shows COMT rs4680 AG with a four-dot evidence indicator.
resultRows.plainEnglishMeaning: Observed public Mood & Brain sample-report structure only. Keep this separate from local genome evidence; it is not a personal mental-health, mood, addiction, eating-disorder, ADHD, dyslexia, cognitive, therapy, sleep, medication, supplement, school/work, treatment, or medical recommendation.

resultRows.geneticAnalysis: Visible rows include Low Mood impact 3/5 evidence 3/5 and Attention impact 4/5 evidence 3/5. The sample says poor sleep may worsen depression and short sleep duration is linked to attention problems and ADHD; it also includes a COMT rs4680 AG sample row for attention.
resultRows.plainEnglishMeaning: Observed public Mood & Brain sample-report structure only. Keep this separate from local genome evidence; it is not a personal mental-health, mood, addiction, eating-disorder, ADHD, dyslexia, cognitive, therapy, sleep, medication, supplement, school/work, treatment, or medical recommendation.

neurobehavioralContext.plainEnglishMeaning: The safe output for this package is an evidence-availability report plus clearly separated public sample-report structure. It explains which model, clinical, cognitive, medication, pediatric, school/work, and professional inputs are missing and avoids mental-health or cognitive conclusions from missing evidence.
unsupportedClaims: Diagnosis, current mental-state assessment, personal mental-health risk, mood state, ADHD, dyslexia, addiction risk, eating-disorder risk, cognitive decline, brain fog, creativity, memory, executive function, treatment response, medication need, supplement need, therapy need, sleep intervention, school/work action, pediatric action, all-clear reassurance, and clinical actionability are not supported by this fixture.
Public sample rows are included only for output-format parity and citation binding. They are not local risk estimates, diagnoses, treatment recommendations, or local genotype findings.
neurobehavioralContext.professionalReview: Use this as a local-agent readiness fixture only. Mental-health symptoms, cognitive concerns, medication decisions, school/work decisions, and health actions require validated test scope, relevant clinical context, and qualified professional review.

professionalReview: Keep this output educational and non-diagnostic.
Do not use this fixture to start, stop, or change testing, screening, medication, supplements, therapy, sleep treatment, school/work decisions, or treatment.
Use qualified professional review for mental-health symptoms, cognitive concerns, addiction concerns, medication questions, or health decisions.

Symptoms, current mental state, cognitive testing, neurologic exam, biomarkers, imaging, labs, medication context, pediatric context, family history, school or work context, and professional interpretation are unavailable.

uncertainty: No calibrated mental-health or mood model, ancestry applicability metadata, clinical context, professional review, medication response model, current mental-state assessment, cognitive testing, or local risk calculation is supplied.
The public sample PDF supports output structure and citation binding only; sample percentiles, likelihood labels, variants, and recommendation ratings must not be interpreted as local results.
Absence of evidence in this fixture must be reported as unavailable, not converted into low risk or reassurance.
limitations: This generated result does not include raw genome data and does not emit VCF, FASTQ, BAM, or CRAM content.

Reference resources used for boundary language and sample structure: CDC Genetic Testing; MedlinePlus Genetics; MedlinePlus Genetics: complex disorders; FDA Direct-to-Consumer Tests; NHGRI overview of polygenic risk scores; GWAS Catalog; NIMH: Looking at My Genes; Mood & Brain DNA Report sample report.
The result is a deterministic synthetic validation artifact for local-agent packaging, not a medical interpretation.
